items.title: Xavi Simons ruled out of rest of season and World Cup with ruptured ACL
items.summary: ‘Heartbroken’ Tottenham midfielder stretchered off in win at Wolves Netherlands star faces eight months out after scans confirm injury Xavi Simons has ruptured his anterior cruciate ligament and will be out for around eight months. The Tottenham midfielder suffered the injury in his team’s 1-0 win at Wolves on Saturday and will be unavailable for the remainder of the club’s Premier League survival fight. His devastation has been compounded by the knowledge that he will not be able to play for the Netherlands at the World Cup finals this summer. Simons was stretchered off at Molineux in the 63rd minute after twisting his knee in the turf as he chased a ball towards the byline. It is a terrible blow for him and the club, whose new manager, Roberto De Zerbi, was counting on the 23-year-old’s creativity in the battle against relegation. Despite the victory over Wolves, which was Spurs’ first in 16 league games, they remain 18th in the table, two points behind 17th-placed West Ham with four matches to play. Continue reading...

items.title: Vrees wordt werkelijkheid: Xavi Simons mist WK door zware blessure
items.summary: Xavi Simons zal niet aanwezig zijn op het WK in de Verenigde Staten, Mexico en Canada. De Oranje-international liep bij Tottenham een zware knieblessure op en heeft op sociale media bekendgemaakt dat hij het WK moet missen.

items.title: 'Four days of hell in hospital' - Kearns on miscarriage
items.summary: Aston Villa and England midfielder Missy Bo Kearns says she spent "four days of hell in hospital" after suffering a miscarriage and contracting sepsis.

items.title: Bajraktarevic schittert met twee goals en twee assists in PSV-masterclass
items.summary: PSV speelde deze speelronde al op donderdagavond maar dat zorgde niet voor veel verwarring bij de kersverse landskampioen. Aan de hand van Esmir Bajraktarevic liepen de Eindhovenaren in eigen huis uit naar een klinkende zege op PEC Zwolle.

items.title: Ueda op schot tegen Groningen, piepjonge debutant schrijft geschiedenis
items.summary: Feyenoord deed zaterdagmiddag goede zaken in de strijd om plek twee in de Eredivisie. Met een eenvoudige zege op FC Groningen legden de Rotterdammers de druk weer bij de concurrentie. Ayase Ueda maakte er twee, maar na afloop ging het toch vooral over de piepjonge debutant van Feyenoord: Ilai Grootfaam (16) mocht in de tweede helft invallen.

items.title: Godts grote man bij Ajax op bezoek bij NAC
items.summary: Ajax deed zaterdagavond goede zaken in de strijd om de Champions League-tickets in de Eredivisie. De Amsterdammers wonnen op bezoek bij NAC Breda, aan de hand van een swingende Mika Godts. Met name de goal van de jonge Belg was om door een ringetje te halen.

items.title: Farioli telt af richting titel en reageert op Chelsea-geruchten
items.summary: Francesco Farioli is met een nipte uitzege op Estrela da Amadora weer een stap dichter bij de landstitel met FC Porto. De voormalig Ajax-trainer reageerde bij Sport TV op de overwinning en de geruchten rondom de interesse van Chelsea.

items.title: Drowning in the banter-sphere: how can the Premier League rivals handle the heat? | Barney Ronay
items.summary: The current season has become a meme-war without end, an endless rolling wall of gloat and taunt in which players and managers must try to block out the noise In his new book, Saved, Gianluigi Buffon talks about feeling crushed by nerves even at the peak of his playing career. The day before the 2006 World Cup final Buffon and Gennaro Gattuso walked past the French squad after training and were immediately sent into a tailspin by their opponents’ intimidating size and athleticism. “We don’t stand a chance,” Gattuso joked, not actually joking. Buffon spent most of the night smoking in the hotel corridor with half the Italy team. At breakfast nobody could speak. They turned up at the stadium already feeling exhausted. Continue reading...

items.title: Will doubters believe in Hearts' title chances now?
items.summary: Some have waited all season for Hearts to falter. Plenty thought it might be at Easter Road. But Derek McInnes' side are still going, writes Tom English.

items.title: Arsenal 'on a mission' - is another special season on the horizon?
items.summary: The Gunners are one game away from a second successive Women's Champions League final, and are also still in the Women's Super League title race.

items.title: McFarlane delighted Chelsea stopped the rot by beating Leeds in FA Cup semi-final
items.summary: ‘I had a feel for what they needed psychologically,’ he says Daniel Farke admits nerves got the better of Leeds Chelsea’s interim manager Calum McFarlane admitted they had to “break the momentum” of five straight Premier League defeats without scoring as they held off Leeds to reach the FA Cup final. A header in the first half from Enzo Fernández proved decisive as Daniel Farke’s side were frustrated by several fine saves from the Chelsea goalkeeper, Robert Sánchez. It means McFarlane, who replaced Liam Rosenior following his sacking this week, will have a chance to win Chelsea’s first domestic trophy since 2018 when he takes charge of next month’s final against Manchester City. Continue reading...

items.title: Hire, fire, final - Chelsea's player power may be ugly, but it wins
items.summary: Chelsea are in another FA Cup final - but only after their players showed up in a way they were no longer doing for Liam Rosenior, writes Phil McNulty.

items.title: Fernandez fires Chelsea to FA Cup final showdown with Man City
items.summary: Enzo Fernandez' first-half header gives Chelsea a 1-0 win over Leeds United in the semi-final of the FA Cup at Wembley Stadium.

items.title: Hearts leave it late to sink nine-man Hibernian and capitalise on Rangers loss
items.summary: The greatest myth relating to this Scottish football season is that a country eagerly awaits a Hearts title win. For supporters of Celtic and Rangers, the league flag flying at Tynecastle Park for the first time since 1960 might just be palatable on the basis of no bragging rights in Glasgow. Others have no desire whatsoever to see Hearts become the team to break a four-decade duopoly. This is a tribal football country and unashamedly so. Case in point was delivered here by a diminished Hibernian, whose supporters demanded they fight tooth and nail to burst the Hearts bubble. Emigration will appeal to those with a fondness for the green half of Edinburgh should the Hearts fairytale reach appropriate conclusion. It is now only four games from that point. Scottish football is delivering the most thrilling top-flight race in Europe, with the underdogs still ahead of Glasgow’s big two. Continue reading...

items.title: Olivia Smith’s late strike gives Arsenal edge over OL Lyonnes in WCL semi-final
items.summary: A calamitous defensive error from the goalkeeper Christiane Endler helped reigning champions Arsenal to come from behind against OL Lyonnes and ensure they take a narrow lead into Saturday’s Champions League semi-final second leg in France. “It’s only half-time,” said the manager Renée Slegers, urging caution before the return in Lyon. Continue reading...

items.title: "J'ai pris énormément de plaisir" : Jelle Vossen savoure son grand retour avec Zulte Waregem
items.summary: Zulte Waregem n'a fait qu'une bouchée de la RAAL ce dimanche soir. Une victoire nette et sans bavure 4-0.

items.title: Jelle Vossen genoot van terugkeer en zege tegen La Louvière
items.summary: Zulte Waregem won zondagavond gemakkelijk van La Louvière. Het werd maar liefst 4-0 in de Elindus Arena.

items.title: Le Bayern Munich de Vincent Kompany aura du pain sur la planche : le PSG est presque au complet
items.summary: Le PSG récupère ses cadres avant d'affronter le Bayern Munich de Vincent Kompany. Hakimi, Mendes et Ruiz vont bien.

items.title: Meningen lopen uiteen over kritisch KVM-publiek: "Dansen niet op tafel" vs "Liever met mooi voetbal in Play-off 2?"
items.summary: Na de 1-4-nederlaag tegen STVV scandeerde het KVM-publiek dat het voetbal wil zien en daarnaast ook 'bloed, zweet en tranen'. Taferelen die je eerder verwacht bij een laaggerangschikte ploeg dan bij een team uit Play-off 1, ook al valt de puntenoogst daar fel tegen.

items.title: "Tuer un match comme ça..." : Nathan Saliba conteste le rouge de Killian Sardella
items.summary: Anderlecht a craqué face à l'Union Saint-Gilloise malgré une bonne première période. L'exclusion de Killian Sardella a tout changé.

items.title: Spitsenprobleem bij Gent? "Tijdens de zomer kunnen we conclusies trekken"
items.summary: Gent verloor zaterdag de derby tegen Club Brugge. De mannen van Rik De Mil konden voor de derde wedstrijd op rij niet scoren...

items.title: Jérémy Taravel zag Anderlecht de evenknie zijn van Union: "In eerste helft waren we zelfs beter"
items.summary: Na de 1-3-nederlaag tegen Union Saint-Gilloise hing bij Anderlecht vooral een wrang gevoel in de rangen. Coach Jérémy Taravel zag zijn ploeg immers een heel degelijke eerste helft spelen, ondanks de achterstand.

items.title: La pire équipe des Champions' Play-Offs : Fred Vanderbiest explose après la débâcle de Malines
items.summary: Battu par Saint-Trond, Malines est dernier des Championss Play-Offs. Déçu par la prestation de son équipe, Fred Vanderbiest pointe un manque de solidité et de mental.

items.title: Voormalig Anderlecht-speler beslissend in Griekse bekerfinale
items.summary: Aaron Leya Iseka is tegenwoordig actief in de Griekse competitie. Daar heeft hij zonet de allereerste trofee van zijn carrière gewonnen.

items.title: "Celui qui tombe en premier a gagné..." : l'expulsion de Sardella excessive ?
items.summary: Killian Sardella devait-il être exclu pour son geste à l'encontre de Guilherme Smith ? Les avis divergent, et naturellement, dépendent de la couleur du maillot interrogé.

items.title: Fitzpatrick brothers hold on for historic Zurich Classic victory
items.summary: Matt Fitzpatrick and his younger brother Alex overcame a nervy back nine to clinch a historic victory at the Zurich Classic of New Orleans, one which earns the latter a two-year exemption on the PGA Tour.

items.title: O'Sullivan thumps table as Higgins fights back in Crucible clash
items.summary: Ronnie O'Sullivan thumped the table in frustration as John Higgins fought back in the second session of their second round match at the World Snooker Championship on Sunday night.

items.title: Norrie wins to set up first career meeting with Sinner in Madrid Open
items.summary: Cameron Norrie set up a first ever meeting with Jannik Sinner after battling into the fourth round of the Madrid Open with a 7-5 7-6 (7-5) victory over Thiago Agustin Tirante on Sunday.

items.title: Daryz given Royal Ascot target after impressive Prix Ganay victory
items.summary: Francis-Henri Graffard will target Royal Ascot with Daryz after seeing his Arc hero make a foot perfect start to the season in the Prix Ganay at ParisLongchamp.

items.title: Root falls four short of century in first knock of season for Yorkshire
items.summary: Joe Root quickly found form on his first appearance of the summer for Yorkshire duty, but fell four short of a century on day three of their County Championship clash with Sussex at Headingley.
